source: ABT Bank '5/6, 7/6 & 10/6 SOFR ARM Disclosure (Conforming & Jumbo loans)' (https://www.abt.bank/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Early-ARM-Disclosure-5yr-7yr-and-10yr-ARM-SOFR-Static.pdf), captured 2026-04-30 to tests/fixtures/arm/oracle/abt_bank_5_6_sofr_disclosure_2022.pdf (SHA256 891e70b7bc9de8a9804f53f32d1cb05488e381dd9b147bcd76d466555da2d83b). Substituted for AmericU 5/6 SOFR ARM Program Disclosure per Plan 05-06 Rule-4 deviation (T-05-34 URL rot — AmericU URL returns HTTP 404 and apex domain is parked).
notes: 5/6 SOFR ARM C30 (Conforming, 30-year). Worked example uses a $10,000 loan size for clear per-$10k math. Disclosure publishes only the Initial Interest Rate + Initial Monthly Payment row and the Maximum Interest Rate + Maximum Monthly Payment row (the worst-case path under the 2/1/5 cap structure). The 'Loan Year in Which Max Payment May Be Reached' is the 7th year. CONVENTION NOTE: the disclosure's $90.54 'Maximum Monthly Payment' is the payment for a fresh $10,000 loan fully amortized over 360 months at the lifetime-cap rate (10.375%) — i.e., the regulatory worst-case figure showing the highest payment the borrower could ever theoretically face. It is NOT the actual cash-flow payment the engine produces at month 79, because by then the engine has paid the loan down per Phase 5 D-05 'full-remaining-term re-amortization' (so re-amortizing the then-current balance over the remaining 282 months at 10.375% yields a lower nominal payment). The cross-source test in tests/test_arm.py::test_oracle_cross_validation_5_6 honours both conventions: engine matches ABT exactly under the disclosure's full-original-term convention. Decimal-string only per CLAUDE.md money discipline.

expected_per_period._provenance: ABT disclosure 'Initial Monthly Payment' row, 5/6 ARM C30 column ($56.00 per $10,000)

ABT disclosure 'Maximum Monthly Payment' row, 5/6 ARM C30 column ($90.54 per $10,000); reached at the 4th change date (month 79 = month 61 + 3*6) when worst-case path hits the 5pp lifetime cap (5.375% + 5.000% = 10.375%); within the 7th loan year per disclosure 'Loan Year in Which Max Payment May Be Reached' row
